cards.title: Methylation Cycle — Clinical Overview

alerts.label: Why methylation matters
alerts.text: Methylation occurs 1 billion times per second in every cell. It controls: DNA repair, gene expression (epigenetics), neurotransmitter synthesis and degradation, detoxification (Phase II), oestrogen metabolism, immune regulation, and myelin formation.

pearls.label: 🟡 MTHFR polymorphism — practical guide
pearls.text: MTHFR C677T homozygous (TT) = 70% reduced enzyme activity. Present in 10% of population. NOT a disease — a polymorphism requiring adequate cofactors. Management: 5-methyltetrahydrofolate (5-MTHF, NOT folic acid), methylcobalamin (B12), B2, B6. Check: serum homocysteine (target <10 µmol/L), RBC folate, active B12. Avoid high-dose folic acid — can paradoxically worsen methylation by blocking MTHFR competitively.

cards.title: Homocysteine — Interpretation & Management

rows: Medical review + above + investigate CBS deficiency

pearls.label: 🟡 Betaine — the backup methylator
pearls.text: Trimethylglycine (betaine, from beetroot) provides an alternative methylation pathway that does NOT require MTHFR. 2–6g/day lowers homocysteine by 10–20% independently of B-vitamins. Especially useful when MTHFR is highly impaired. Found naturally in: beetroot ★, spinach, quinoa, wheat germ.

rows: Endogenous; NAC precursor; liposomal GSH supp.
Master antioxidant, Phase II detox, regenerates other antioxidants

Mitochondrial electron transport, lipid peroxidation prevention

pearls.label: 🟡 Sulforaphane — most potent Nrf2 activator
pearls.text: Sulforaphane from broccoli sprouts activates Nrf2 → turns on 200+ cytoprotective genes for 72 hours from a single dose. 3-day-old broccoli sprouts contain 20–50× more sulforaphane than mature broccoli. Daily 50g sprouts = clinical dose. Myrosinase enzyme (from mustard, horseradish, or light steaming then cooling) converts glucoraphanin → sulforaphane — raw sprouts + mustard seed = optimal delivery.

cards.title: Mitochondrial Health — Clinical Assessment & Support

alerts.label: Mitochondrial Dysfunction — The Root of Fatigue
alerts.text: Mitochondria produce 95% of cellular ATP. Mitochondrial dysfunction underlies: chronic fatigue syndrome, fibromyalgia, ageing, neurodegeneration, metabolic syndrome, and many chronic diseases. Unlike acute disease, mitochondrial dysfunction is subtle, pervasive, and highly responsive to nutritional intervention.

rows: Elevated in mitochondrial dysfunction — impaired oxidative phosphorylation
Krebs cycle intermediates elevated: citrate, isocitrate, succinate, fumarate, malate — indicates bottlenecks in TCA cycle
Specialist lab. Genova Diagnostics, Great Plains Laboratory.
Ubiquinol form preferred. Low in: statin use, ageing, heart failure, CFS
>0.8 µg/mL. Supplement to >2.5 µg/mL in clinical conditions.

Negative. Elevated in: CFS, autoimmune, post-viral syndromes.

steps.title: Mitochondrial Support Protocol
steps.text: CoQ10 ubiquinol 200–400mg/day ★★★ (most evidence-based mitochondrial supplement — active reduced form, superior to ubiquinone in compromised patients). PQQ (pyrroloquinoline quinone) 20mg/day ★★ (stimulates mitochondrial biogenesis — new mitochondria, not just function). NAD+ precursors: NR (nicotinamide riboside) or NMN 250–500mg/day ★★★ (NAD+ declines 50% by age 60 — essential for ETC complex I). L-Carnitine or Acetyl-L-carnitine 1–2g/day ★★★ (shuttles fatty acids into mitochondria for beta-oxidation). R-Alpha lipoic acid 300–600mg/day ★★★ (TCA cofactor + antioxidant + regenerates CoQ10/E/C). Magnesium malate 400mg ★★★ (malate = TCA intermediate, Mg cofactor for ATP synthase).

steps.title: Lifestyle Mitochondrial Biogenesis
steps.text: Exercise ★★★ — single most powerful mitochondrial biogenesis stimulus (PGC-1α activation). Zone 2 aerobic (150 min/week) + HIIT 2×/week. Fasting ★★★ — AMPK activation triggers mitophagy (clearing damaged mitochondria) + biogenesis. Cold exposure ★★ — cold thermogenesis activates UCP1 + PGC-1α (cold showers, cryotherapy). Ketogenic diet ★★ (ketones = cleaner mitochondrial fuel than glucose — less ROS per ATP generated). Sleep ★★★ — mitochondrial repair and biogenesis predominantly occur in deep sleep.

cards.title: Magnesium — The Master Mineral

alerts.label: Magnesium — Cofactor for 300+ Enzymatic Reactions
alerts.text: Magnesium is the fourth most abundant mineral in the body and a cofactor for over 300 enzymes. It is essential for: ATP synthesis, DNA repair, protein synthesis, neuromuscular transmission, glucose metabolism, blood pressure regulation, and vitamin D activation. Deficiency is estimated in 60–70% of adults eating a Western diet.

rows: Sleep, anxiety, muscle relaxation, PMS, general deficiency. Best tolerated form — no laxative effect.
Cognitive function, memory, ADHD, neurological conditions, Alzheimer's prevention
1000–2000mg MgT (provides ~50–100mg elemental). Expensive but neurologically targeted.
Fibromyalgia ★★★, chronic fatigue, muscle pain, energy metabolism (malate = TCA cycle intermediate)
Constipation, kidney stone prevention (citrate inhibits oxalate crystallisation)
Cheap but ineffective — avoid for systemic deficiency. Useful only as a laxative or antacid.
Topical foot soaks, muscle cramps (some benefit reported). Evidence weaker than oral.
Epsom salt (MgSO4) foot bath — relaxing but uncertain systemic absorption

pearls.label: 🟡 Drugs That Deplete Magnesium
pearls.text: PPIs ★★★ — most significant Mg depletion. Severe hypomagnesaemia can occur with >3 months PPI use (FDA black box warning). Check serum Mg in all long-term PPI patients. Thiazide and loop diuretics ★★★ — renal Mg wasting (tubular). Aminoglycosides, cisplatin (renally depleting). Alcohol ★★★ — inhibits renal Mg reabsorption. Beta-agonists (salbutamol) — shift Mg intracellularly. These patients need routine Mg monitoring and supplementation.

steps.title: Clinical Deficiency Patterns by Condition
steps.text: Migraine: Mg 400–600mg/day ★★★ — serum Mg low in 50% of migraine patients during attack. IV Mg terminates acute migraine in 50% (comparable to triptans). Cardiovascular: Mg 400mg ★★★ — reduces AF, ventricular arrhythmia, hypertension, torsades de pointes. T2DM: Mg 400mg ★★★ — deficiency = 10× ↑ T2DM risk. Insulin resistance is worsened by Mg deficiency (insulin receptor requires Mg). Insomnia: Mg glycinate 400mg at bedtime ★★★ — regulates GABA-A receptor, melatonin production. PMS: Mg 360mg from day 15 of cycle ★★★ — reduces mood symptoms, cramping, breast tenderness.

steps.title: Testing Magnesium Status
steps.text: Serum Mg: poor indicator (only 1% of Mg is extracellular — serum appears normal until severely deficient). RBC Mg (red blood cell intracellular Mg): better indicator of tissue stores. 24-hour urine Mg: measures Mg retention (low retention = deficiency if kidneys retaining Mg). Ionic Mg (most accurate): specialist lab. Practical approach: assess clinically — muscle cramps, eye twitching, anxiety, insomnia, constipation, fatigue, headaches, palpitations = clinical deficiency indicators. RBC Mg if available. Treat empirically in most cases — Mg glycinate is safe and well tolerated.

cards.title: Zinc — Clinical Deep Dive

alerts.label: Zinc — The Most Versatile Immune and Metabolic Mineral
alerts.text: Zinc is involved in: 300+ enzyme reactions, DNA synthesis, protein production, immune function (thymulin synthesis, T-cell maturation), testosterone production, insulin signalling, wound healing, taste and smell (carbonic anhydrase VI in saliva), visual cycle (retinal function), and antioxidant (superoxide dismutase cofactor). Deficiency is extremely common and profoundly multisystemic.

rows: ↓ Thymulin (thymus hormone) → ↓ T-cell maturation and function. ↓ NK cell activity.
Zinc-dependent carbonic anhydrase VI in saliva and olfactory neuroepithelium. Classic presentation of zinc deficiency.
Zinc required for nail keratin synthesis. White transverse bands = Muehrcke's lines (hypoalbuminaemia). White spots = zinc deficiency classically.
Zinc required for metalloproteinases (tissue remodelling), collagen synthesis, and inflammatory phase of wound healing.
Zinc reduces 5α-reductase (DHT conversion), inhibits Propionibacterium acnes. Comparable to tetracycline in RCTs.
Zinc is cofactor for testosterone synthesis. Zinc deficiency = low LH + low testosterone — both reverse with correction.
Zinc required for IGF-1 signalling. Most common cause of growth faltering in developing countries after malnutrition.

steps.title: Zinc Forms — Clinical Selection
steps.text: Zinc picolinate: best absorbed form. Picolinic acid chelate = efficient membrane transport. Good for: immune, acne, testosterone. Zinc bisglycinate: second-best absorbed. Gentlest on stomach. Best for daily maintenance. Zinc carnosine: specific for gastric mucosal protection. PepZin GI — clinically proven for gastric ulcers, H.pylori support, gut barrier. Zinc citrate: moderate absorption. Good tolerability. Zinc gluconate: widely available. Moderate absorption. Zinc lozenges for cold prevention/treatment (ionic zinc) ★★★ — must dissolve in mouth to work (not swallow). Zinc oxide: poorly absorbed — topical use only (sunscreen, nappy cream). Do NOT use for deficiency.

steps.title: Zinc:Copper Balance — Critical Safety Point
steps.text: Zinc supplementation >40mg/day for >8 weeks induces metallothionein in gut enterocytes — which binds copper and prevents absorption. Chronic high-dose zinc → copper deficiency: anaemia (sideropeic + microcytic, not responding to iron), leukopenia, peripheral neuropathy (myelopathy), neurological symptoms (mimics B12 deficiency). Clinical rule: add 1mg copper for every 15mg zinc above baseline. Monitor: check caeruloplasmin (copper transport protein) or direct copper if supplementing zinc long-term at therapeutic doses (>30mg/day). Do NOT co-supplement copper with zinc at the same time — separate by 2–4 hours.

rows: Serum B12 normal but functional deficiency common
Hb normal but ferritin <30 = functional deficiency
Serum zinc unreliable — redistributes in inflammation
Serum Mg normal in 80% of deficient patients

pearls.label: 🟡 Ferritin — the most underdiagnosed deficiency
pearls.text: Laboratory "normal" ferritin starts at 12 µg/L. Functional iron deficiency symptoms (fatigue, hair loss, cold intolerance, brain fog, restless legs) occur at ferritin <30 µg/L — a level most labs report as normal. Target ferritin 50–100 µg/L for symptom resolution. Iron bisglycinate (25mg/day with vitamin C, away from tea/coffee) is the best-tolerated form. Recheck in 3 months.

cards.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids — Complete Clinical Guide

alerts.label: Omega-3 — The Most Evidence-Based Supplement in Naturopathic Practice
alerts.text: Omega-3 PUFAs (EPA, DHA, ALA) have the broadest evidence base of any supplement — cardiovascular, neurological, inflammatory, psychiatric, and metabolic benefits confirmed across thousands of RCTs and meta-analyses.

rows: Converts to EPA/DHA at only 5–10% efficiency — insufficient as sole source
Oily fish, fish oil, algae oil
Oily fish, fish oil, algae oil
Brain structure, retina, fetal development, cognitive health
★★★★ REDUCE-IT trial: icosapentaenoic acid 4g ↓ CV events 25%
★★★★ Meta-analysis: EPA superior to DHA for depression
★★★★ Critical — DHA is the primary structural fatty acid in fetal brain
★★★★ Multiple RCTs — reduces TNF-α, IL-1, LTB4
★★★★ Meta-analysis 16 RCTs — modest but consistent benefit
★★★ Good for prevention — less for treatment of established dementia

pearls.label: 🟡 Fish Oil vs Algae Oil
pearls.text: Algae oil is the original source — fish accumulate DHA/EPA by eating algae. Advantages of algae oil over fish oil: (1) Sustainable — no overfishing concern. (2) Vegan/vegetarian appropriate. (3) No mercury or PCB contamination risk. (4) No fishy aftertaste or burps. (5) DHA content often higher. Disadvantage: EPA content is lower in most algae oils — choose high-EPA algae oil for anti-inflammatory use. Oxidation check for fish oil: open the capsule — if it smells rancid (strong, unpleasant fishy smell), it is oxidised and potentially pro-inflammatory. Store in refrigerator, check expiry, take with food.

cards.title: Vitamin D — The Master Hormone

alerts.label: Vitamin D — Not Just a Vitamin
alerts.text: Vitamin D is a secosteroid hormone — its receptor (VDR) is present in virtually every cell. It regulates over 200 genes involved in immunity, cell differentiation, inflammation, insulin secretion, blood pressure regulation, and calcium homeostasis. Deficiency is a global pandemic — affecting 1 billion people.

rows: Rickets (children), osteomalacia (adults), muscle weakness, bone pain, fractures
Reduced immunity, fatigue, depression risk, muscle weakness, impaired calcium absorption
Suboptimal — bone density, immune function, mood may be affected
Target range for most clinical benefits. Cancer prevention, immune function, cardiovascular
Hypercalcaemia, hypercalciuria. Rare — requires very high supplementation (>40,000 IU/day chronic)
Under supervision — target 125–175 nmol/L (Coimbra protocol in MS, vitiligo)

pearls.label: 🟡 D3 + K2 + Mg — The Triad
pearls.text: Vitamin D3 should always be combined with: K2 (MK-7) 100–200mcg — directs calcium to bone (osteocalcin carboxylation) and arteries (matrix Gla protein activation), preventing vascular calcification from high-dose Vit D. Magnesium 300–400mg — required for Vit D activation (25-OH-D → 1,25-OH2-D requires Mg-dependent enzyme CYP27B1). Low magnesium blocks Vit D conversion — patients who take Vit D without Mg may not respond adequately. D3 without K2 at high doses can raise serum calcium and deposit it in soft tissues — always prescribe together.

🟡 D3 + K2 + Mg — The Triad
Vitamin D3 should always be combined with: K2 (MK-7) 100–200mcg — directs calcium to bone (osteocalcin carboxylation) and arteries (matrix Gla protein activation), preventing vascular calcification from high-dose Vit D. Magnesium 300–400mg — required for Vit D activation (25-OH-D → 1,25-OH2-D requires Mg-dependent enzyme CYP27B1). Low magnesium blocks Vit D conversion — patients who take Vit D without Mg may not respond adequately. D3 without K2 at high doses can raise serum calcium and deposit it in soft tissues — always prescribe together.

pearls.label: 🟡 Haemochromatosis — Iron Overload
pearls.text: Hereditary haemochromatosis (HFE gene mutation — C282Y homozygous) causes progressive iron overload → liver cirrhosis, cardiomyopathy, diabetes, arthropathy, hypogonadism, bronze skin. Most common genetic disorder in people of Northern European descent (1:200). Screen with transferrin saturation (>45% → HFE gene test). Treatment: therapeutic phlebotomy 500ml every 1–2 weeks. NEVER prescribe iron supplements without checking ferritin and transferrin saturation first — giving iron to a patient with undiagnosed haemochromatosis causes serious harm.

steps.title: Enhancers of Iron Absorption
steps.text: Vitamin C 100–200mg with iron supplement — increases absorption 2–4x (reduces Fe³⁺ to Fe²⁺). Meat factor (haem iron) — enhances non-haem iron absorption alongside. Cooking in cast-iron cookware — leaches iron into food. Acidic foods and liquids — stomach acid required for non-haem absorption.

steps.title: Inhibitors of Iron Absorption
steps.text: Phytates (whole grains, legumes) — reduce absorption 50–65%. Polyphenols/tannins (tea, coffee, red wine) — reduce 60–90% — avoid 1hr before and 2hrs after iron. Calcium — competes for same intestinal transporter — separate by 2hrs. PPIs and antacids — reduce gastric acid needed for non-haem iron conversion. Excessive zinc supplementation — competitive inhibition.

cards.title: Nutrient Absorption — Optimising Bioavailability

alerts.label: Bioavailability — What You Eat Is Not What You Absorb
alerts.text: The gap between dietary intake and cellular uptake of nutrients is critical in naturopathic practice. Factors affecting absorption: gut health, stomach acid, food combinations, supplement form, timing, and individual genetics all profoundly influence nutrient bioavailability.

pearls.label: 🟡 Low Stomach Acid (Hypochlorhydria) — The Hidden Epidemic
pearls.text: Hypochlorhydria (low gastric HCl) impairs absorption of: iron, B12, calcium, zinc, folate, and protein (requires pepsin activation by acid). Common causes: PPI use (most common), H. pylori infection, atrophic gastritis (elderly), stress, zinc deficiency (zinc needed for HCl production). Signs: bloating after meals, belching, undigested food in stool, multiple nutrient deficiencies despite supplementation. Test: Betaine HCl challenge (150–500mg with protein meal — if no warmth/burning, hypochlorhydria likely). Support: apple cider vinegar 1 tbsp in water before protein meals, digestive bitters, Betaine HCl with pepsin supplements.

cards.title: B Vitamins — Complete Clinical Guide

rows: Pyruvate dehydrogenase cofactor — glucose → energy. Nerve membrane maintenance.
Wet beriberi (cardiac, oedema). Dry beriberi (peripheral neuropathy). Wernicke's encephalopathy (alcoholics) — MEDICAL EMERGENCY: confusion + ophthalmoplegia + ataxia.

Wernicke's: IV thiamine 500mg TID × 3 days BEFORE glucose (giving glucose first precipitates Wernicke's in deficient patients). Maintenance: 50–100mg/day. High-risk: alcoholics, bariatric surgery, anorexia.
FAD/FMN cofactor — electron transport. Activates B6, folate, B12. Antioxidant (glutathione reductase).
Angular cheilitis, magenta tongue, photophobia, seborrhoea, normocytic anaemia. Worsens with oral contraceptives (common depletion).
Migraine prophylaxis ★★★ 400mg/day — mitochondrial energy enhancement. Maintenance: 10–50mg. Food: eggs, liver, dairy, almonds.
NAD+/NADP+ (central metabolism). Niacin: vasodilation, lipids. Niacinamide: DNA repair, longevity (NAD+ precursor), skin barrier.
Pellagra: 4 Ds — Dermatitis (sun-exposed), Diarrhoea, Dementia, Death. Common in: corn-dependent diets, alcoholism, Hartnup disease.

Niacin (flush): lipids — 1–3g/day (cardiovascular). Niacinamide (no-flush): acne 4–5% topical ★★★, anxiety 500–1000mg, NAD+ support (combined with NR). Tryptophan → niacin (60:1 ratio — 60mg tryptophan = 1mg niacin).
Coenzyme A synthesis — fatty acid metabolism, steroid hormone synthesis, acetylcholine. "Anti-stress vitamin" — adrenal support.
Burning feet syndrome. Deficiency rare — found in most foods. Seen in: severe malnutrition, alcoholism, some anti-HIV drugs.
HPA axis support: 500mg BD. Adrenal recovery protocol. Wound healing (topical dexpanthenol — Bepanthen). Acne: pantothenic acid 10g/day in some protocols (large dose).
Amino acid metabolism, neurotransmitter synthesis (serotonin, dopamine, GABA, histamine). PLP (active P5P form) essential for 100+ enzymes.
Depression, peripheral neuropathy (painful), glossitis, dermatitis, microcytic anaemia. Depleted by: OCP ★★★, isoniazid, penicillamine, theophylline.

OCP-related depression/PMS: P5P 25–50mg BD ★★★. Morning sickness in pregnancy: B6 25mg TID ★★★ (first-line WHO recommendation). Neurotransmitter support: P5P (active form — superior to pyridoxine). Toxicity: >200mg/day long-term → peripheral neuropathy (paradoxically). Use P5P not pyridoxine for chronic use.
Carboxylase cofactor — fatty acid synthesis, gluconeogenesis. Gene expression regulation. Keratin production.
Hair thinning (most common presenting complaint — though biotin deficiency is overdiagnosed). Brittle nails, seborrhoeic dermatitis, conjunctivitis.

Hair/nail: 2.5–5mg/day. Note: biotin supplementation falsely elevates thyroid tests and troponin on immunoassay — advise patients to stop biotin 48–72hrs before blood tests. Raw egg whites contain avidin which binds biotin (cooking destroys avidin).
1-carbon metabolism, DNA synthesis, methylation. Neural tube development. Homocysteine remethylation.
Megaloblastic anaemia. Neural tube defects (maternal deficiency → spina bifida). Elevated homocysteine. Depression.

Pregnancy prevention: 400–800mcg methylfolate (NOT folic acid in MTHFR — see below). Megaloblastic anaemia: 5mg/day AFTER confirming B12 adequate. MTHFR C677T/A1298C: use L-methylfolate (5-MTHF) not folic acid.
Myelin synthesis (methylcobalamin). DNA synthesis (adenosylcobalamin). Methylation cycle.

Megaloblastic anaemia, subacute combined degeneration of cord (posterior columns + lateral), peripheral neuropathy, depression, cognitive decline. Serum B12 lab range is too low (>150 pmol/L) — functional deficiency at 150–300 pmol/L.

Methylcobalamin preferred (over cyanocobalamin — active form, no cyanide metabolite). Oral: 1000mcg/day. IM hydroxycobalamin: monthly if severe deficiency or absorption failure (atrophic gastritis, gastrectomy, Crohn's, metformin use).

cards.title: Genomic Nutrition — MTHFR & Variants

alerts.label: Nutrigenomics — Personalised Nutrition Based on Genetics
alerts.text: Individual genetic variants (SNPs — single nucleotide polymorphisms) alter nutrient metabolism, requirements, and risk of deficiency-related disease. MTHFR is the most clinically actionable example — but other variants (COMT, APOE, VDR, BCMO1) are increasingly relevant in naturopathic practice.

rows: Methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase — converts folic acid to 5-MTHF (active methylfolate)
Homozygous (TT): enzyme activity ↓ 70%. Heterozygous (CT): ↓ 40%. High homocysteine if folate/B12/B6 insufficient.

Use L-methylfolate (5-MTHF) — NOT folic acid (cannot convert). B12 methylcobalamin. Riboflavin (B2 is MTHFR enzyme cofactor). Check homocysteine. Affects: cardiovascular risk, NTD risk, depression, recurrent miscarriage, treatment-resistant depression.
Less severe effect than C677T alone. Compound heterozygous (C677T + A1298C): significant impairment.
Same approach as C677T. Compound heterozygotes: more aggressive methylfolate protocol. May need B2 co-factor supplementation.
Catechol-O-methyltransferase — degrades catecholamines (dopamine, adrenaline, noradrenaline) and oestrogen metabolites

Val/Val (fast COMT): rapid catecholamine breakdown → less anxious, lower oestrogen metabolites. Met/Met (slow COMT): slow breakdown → higher dopamine (better working memory but higher anxiety), elevated 4-OH oestrogen (breast cancer risk).

Slow COMT + high oestrogen: DIM 200mg, calcium D-glucarate, Vit B2 (COMT requires SAMe and B2 as cofactors). High dopamine from slow COMT: may explain caffeine sensitivity, stress reactivity. Avoid: catechol-rich herbs (green tea, quercetin in excess) in slow COMT.
Apolipoprotein E — lipid transport, neuronal repair, amyloid clearance
APOE4: 1 copy = 3× Alzheimer's risk. 2 copies = 12× risk. Also: ↑ LDL, ↑ cardiovascular risk, ↑ omega-3 requirements (needs more DHA for neurological benefit)

APOE4 carriers: DHA 3g/day (brain protection — APOE4 impairs DHA transport to brain). Curcumin 2g/day (amyloid-clearing). Fasting (ketone bodies bypass impaired glucose metabolism). Avoid: excessive saturated fat (↑ LDL dramatically in APOE4). Ketogenic diet benefit controversial in APOE4.
Vit D receptor — determines responsiveness to Vit D
Multiple VDR variants (FokI, BsmI, TaqI, ApaI). Some variants: ↓ receptor affinity → higher Vit D doses needed for same response. VDR variants associated with: Hashimoto's, MS, T1DM, some cancers.
Patients with VDR variants may need 5000–10,000 IU Vit D to achieve adequate 25(OH)D levels. Test 25(OH)D and titrate by blood level — not by dose alone.

pearls.label: 🟡 MTHFR Testing — Practical Guidance
pearls.text: MTHFR testing: available through NivoFit, HealthifyMe, SRL Diagnostics, and direct-to-consumer services in India. Cost: ₹2000–5000. More meaningful than testing: serum homocysteine (functional marker of methylation pathway efficiency — target <9 µmol/L). Elevated homocysteine + unexplained cardiovascular risk + depression + recurrent miscarriage + treatment-resistant depression = MTHFR testing and methylation protocol beneficial. Patient communication: "Your body needs a pre-activated form of folate — it's like your body's GPS needing the right format of map." Avoid catastrophising — MTHFR is a SNP, not a disease. With appropriate supplementation, outcomes are excellent.

cards.title: Methylation Cycle — Clinical Overview

alerts.label: Why methylation matters
alerts.text: Methylation occurs 1 billion times per second in every cell. It controls: DNA repair, gene expression (epigenetics), neurotransmitter synthesis and degradation, detoxification (Phase II), oestrogen metabolism, immune regulation, and myelin formation.

pearls.label: 🟡 MTHFR polymorphism — practical guide
pearls.text: MTHFR C677T homozygous (TT) = 70% reduced enzyme activity. Present in 10% of population. NOT a disease — a polymorphism requiring adequate cofactors. Management: 5-methyltetrahydrofolate (5-MTHF, NOT folic acid), methylcobalamin (B12), B2, B6. Check: serum homocysteine (target <10 µmol/L), RBC folate, active B12. Avoid high-dose folic acid — can paradoxically worsen methylation by blocking MTHFR competitively.

rows: Medical review + above + investigate CBS deficiency

pearls.label: 🟡 Betaine — the backup methylator
pearls.text: Trimethylglycine (betaine, from beetroot) provides an alternative methylation pathway that does NOT require MTHFR. 2–6g/day lowers homocysteine by 10–20% independently of B-vitamins. Especially useful when MTHFR is highly impaired. Found naturally in: beetroot ★, spinach, quinoa, wheat germ.

cards.title: Mitochondrial Health — Clinical Assessment & Support

alerts.label: Mitochondrial Dysfunction — The Root of Fatigue
alerts.text: Mitochondria produce 95% of cellular ATP. Mitochondrial dysfunction underlies: chronic fatigue syndrome, fibromyalgia, ageing, neurodegeneration, metabolic syndrome, and many chronic diseases. Unlike acute disease, mitochondrial dysfunction is subtle, pervasive, and highly responsive to nutritional intervention.

steps.title: Mitochondrial Support Protocol
steps.text: CoQ10 ubiquinol 200–400mg/day ★★★ (most evidence-based mitochondrial supplement — active reduced form, superior to ubiquinone in compromised patients). PQQ (pyrroloquinoline quinone) 20mg/day ★★ (stimulates mitochondrial biogenesis — new mitochondria, not just function). NAD+ precursors: NR (nicotinamide riboside) or NMN 250–500mg/day ★★★ (NAD+ declines 50% by age 60 — essential for ETC complex I). L-Carnitine or Acetyl-L-carnitine 1–2g/day ★★★ (shuttles fatty acids into mitochondria for beta-oxidation). R-Alpha lipoic acid 300–600mg/day ★★★ (TCA cofactor + antioxidant + regenerates CoQ10/E/C). Magnesium malate 400mg ★★★ (malate = TCA intermediate, Mg cofactor for ATP synthase).

steps.title: Lifestyle Mitochondrial Biogenesis
steps.text: Exercise ★★★ — single most powerful mitochondrial biogenesis stimulus (PGC-1α activation). Zone 2 aerobic (150 min/week) + HIIT 2×/week. Fasting ★★★ — AMPK activation triggers mitophagy (clearing damaged mitochondria) + biogenesis. Cold exposure ★★ — cold thermogenesis activates UCP1 + PGC-1α (cold showers, cryotherapy). Ketogenic diet ★★ (ketones = cleaner mitochondrial fuel than glucose — less ROS per ATP generated). Sleep ★★★ — mitochondrial repair and biogenesis predominantly occur in deep sleep.

pearls.label: 🟡 Haemochromatosis — Iron Overload
pearls.text: Hereditary haemochromatosis (HFE gene mutation — C282Y homozygous) causes progressive iron overload → liver cirrhosis, cardiomyopathy, diabetes, arthropathy, hypogonadism, bronze skin. Most common genetic disorder in people of Northern European descent (1:200). Screen with transferrin saturation (>45% → HFE gene test). Treatment: therapeutic phlebotomy 500ml every 1–2 weeks. NEVER prescribe iron supplements without checking ferritin and transferrin saturation first — giving iron to a patient with undiagnosed haemochromatosis causes serious harm.

steps.title: Enhancers of Iron Absorption
steps.text: Vitamin C 100–200mg with iron supplement — increases absorption 2–4x (reduces Fe³⁺ to Fe²⁺). Meat factor (haem iron) — enhances non-haem iron absorption alongside. Cooking in cast-iron cookware — leaches iron into food. Acidic foods and liquids — stomach acid required for non-haem absorption.

steps.title: Inhibitors of Iron Absorption
steps.text: Phytates (whole grains, legumes) — reduce absorption 50–65%. Polyphenols/tannins (tea, coffee, red wine) — reduce 60–90% — avoid 1hr before and 2hrs after iron. Calcium — competes for same intestinal transporter — separate by 2hrs. PPIs and antacids — reduce gastric acid needed for non-haem iron conversion. Excessive zinc supplementation — competitive inhibition.

alerts.text: The gap between dietary intake and cellular uptake of nutrients is critical in naturopathic practice. Factors affecting absorption: gut health, stomach acid, food combinations, supplement form, timing, and individual genetics all profoundly influence nutrient bioavailability.

pearls.label: 🟡 Low Stomach Acid (Hypochlorhydria) — The Hidden Epidemic
pearls.text: Hypochlorhydria (low gastric HCl) impairs absorption of: iron, B12, calcium, zinc, folate, and protein (requires pepsin activation by acid). Common causes: PPI use (most common), H. pylori infection, atrophic gastritis (elderly), stress, zinc deficiency (zinc needed for HCl production). Signs: bloating after meals, belching, undigested food in stool, multiple nutrient deficiencies despite supplementation. Test: Betaine HCl challenge (150–500mg with protein meal — if no warmth/burning, hypochlorhydria likely). Support: apple cider vinegar 1 tbsp in water before protein meals, digestive bitters, Betaine HCl with pepsin supplements.

cards.title: Magnesium — The Master Mineral

alerts.label: Magnesium — Cofactor for 300+ Enzymatic Reactions
alerts.text: Magnesium is the fourth most abundant mineral in the body and a cofactor for over 300 enzymes. It is essential for: ATP synthesis, DNA repair, protein synthesis, neuromuscular transmission, glucose metabolism, blood pressure regulation, and vitamin D activation. Deficiency is estimated in 60–70% of adults eating a Western diet.

rows: Sleep, anxiety, muscle relaxation, PMS, general deficiency. Best tolerated form — no laxative effect.
Cognitive function, memory, ADHD, neurological conditions, Alzheimer's prevention

pearls.label: 🟡 Drugs That Deplete Magnesium
pearls.text: PPIs ★★★ — most significant Mg depletion. Severe hypomagnesaemia can occur with >3 months PPI use (FDA black box warning). Check serum Mg in all long-term PPI patients. Thiazide and loop diuretics ★★★ — renal Mg wasting (tubular). Aminoglycosides, cisplatin (renally depleting). Alcohol ★★★ — inhibits renal Mg reabsorption. Beta-agonists (salbutamol) — shift Mg intracellularly. These patients need routine Mg monitoring and supplementation.

steps.title: Clinical Deficiency Patterns by Condition
steps.text: Migraine: Mg 400–600mg/day ★★★ — serum Mg low in 50% of migraine patients during attack. IV Mg terminates acute migraine in 50% (comparable to triptans). Cardiovascular: Mg 400mg ★★★ — reduces AF, ventricular arrhythmia, hypertension, torsades de pointes. T2DM: Mg 400mg ★★★ — deficiency = 10× ↑ T2DM risk. Insulin resistance is worsened by Mg deficiency (insulin receptor requires Mg). Insomnia: Mg glycinate 400mg at bedtime ★★★ — regulates GABA-A receptor, melatonin production. PMS: Mg 360mg from day 15 of cycle ★★★ — reduces mood symptoms, cramping, breast tenderness.

steps.title: Testing Magnesium Status
steps.text: Serum Mg: poor indicator (only 1% of Mg is extracellular — serum appears normal until severely deficient). RBC Mg (red blood cell intracellular Mg): better indicator of tissue stores. 24-hour urine Mg: measures Mg retention (low retention = deficiency if kidneys retaining Mg). Ionic Mg (most accurate): specialist lab. Practical approach: assess clinically — muscle cramps, eye twitching, anxiety, insomnia, constipation, fatigue, headaches, palpitations = clinical deficiency indicators. RBC Mg if available. Treat empirically in most cases — Mg glycinate is safe and well tolerated.

cards.title: Zinc — Clinical Deep Dive

alerts.label: Zinc — The Most Versatile Immune and Metabolic Mineral
alerts.text: Zinc is involved in: 300+ enzyme reactions, DNA synthesis, protein production, immune function (thymulin synthesis, T-cell maturation), testosterone production, insulin signalling, wound healing, taste and smell (carbonic anhydrase VI in saliva), visual cycle (retinal function), and antioxidant (superoxide dismutase cofactor). Deficiency is extremely common and profoundly multisystemic.

rows: ↓ Thymulin (thymus hormone) → ↓ T-cell maturation and function. ↓ NK cell activity.
Zinc-dependent carbonic anhydrase VI in saliva and olfactory neuroepithelium. Classic presentation of zinc deficiency.
Zinc required for nail keratin synthesis. White transverse bands = Muehrcke's lines (hypoalbuminaemia). White spots = zinc deficiency classically.
Zinc required for metalloproteinases (tissue remodelling), collagen synthesis, and inflammatory phase of wound healing.
Zinc reduces 5α-reductase (DHT conversion), inhibits Propionibacterium acnes. Comparable to tetracycline in RCTs.
Zinc is cofactor for testosterone synthesis. Zinc deficiency = low LH + low testosterone — both reverse with correction.
Zinc required for IGF-1 signalling. Most common cause of growth faltering in developing countries after malnutrition.

steps.title: Zinc Forms — Clinical Selection
steps.text: Zinc picolinate: best absorbed form. Picolinic acid chelate = efficient membrane transport. Good for: immune, acne, testosterone. Zinc bisglycinate: second-best absorbed. Gentlest on stomach. Best for daily maintenance. Zinc carnosine: specific for gastric mucosal protection. PepZin GI — clinically proven for gastric ulcers, H.pylori support, gut barrier. Zinc citrate: moderate absorption. Good tolerability. Zinc gluconate: widely available. Moderate absorption. Zinc lozenges for cold prevention/treatment (ionic zinc) ★★★ — must dissolve in mouth to work (not swallow). Zinc oxide: poorly absorbed — topical use only (sunscreen, nappy cream). Do NOT use for deficiency.

steps.title: Zinc:Copper Balance — Critical Safety Point
steps.text: Zinc supplementation >40mg/day for >8 weeks induces metallothionein in gut enterocytes — which binds copper and prevents absorption. Chronic high-dose zinc → copper deficiency: anaemia (sideropeic + microcytic, not responding to iron), leukopenia, peripheral neuropathy (myelopathy), neurological symptoms (mimics B12 deficiency). Clinical rule: add 1mg copper for every 15mg zinc above baseline. Monitor: check caeruloplasmin (copper transport protein) or direct copper if supplementing zinc long-term at therapeutic doses (>30mg/day). Do NOT co-supplement copper with zinc at the same time — separate by 2–4 hours.

rows: Serum B12 normal but functional deficiency common

pearls.label: 🟡 Ferritin — the most underdiagnosed deficiency
pearls.text: Laboratory "normal" ferritin starts at 12 µg/L. Functional iron deficiency symptoms (fatigue, hair loss, cold intolerance, brain fog, restless legs) occur at ferritin <30 µg/L — a level most labs report as normal. Target ferritin 50–100 µg/L for symptom resolution. Iron bisglycinate (25mg/day with vitamin C, away from tea/coffee) is the best-tolerated form. Recheck in 3 months.

alerts.label: Omega-3 — The Most Evidence-Based Supplement in Naturopathic Practice

★★★★ Multiple RCTs — reduces TNF-α, IL-1, LTB4
★★★★ Meta-analysis 16 RCTs — modest but consistent benefit
★★★ Good for prevention — less for treatment of established dementia

pearls.label: 🟡 Fish Oil vs Algae Oil
pearls.text: Algae oil is the original source — fish accumulate DHA/EPA by eating algae. Advantages of algae oil over fish oil: (1) Sustainable — no overfishing concern. (2) Vegan/vegetarian appropriate. (3) No mercury or PCB contamination risk. (4) No fishy aftertaste or burps. (5) DHA content often higher. Disadvantage: EPA content is lower in most algae oils — choose high-EPA algae oil for anti-inflammatory use. Oxidation check for fish oil: open the capsule — if it smells rancid (strong, unpleasant fishy smell), it is oxidised and potentially pro-inflammatory. Store in refrigerator, check expiry, take with food.

cards.title: Vitamin D — The Master Hormone

alerts.label: Vitamin D — Not Just a Vitamin
alerts.text: Vitamin D is a secosteroid hormone — its receptor (VDR) is present in virtually every cell. It regulates over 200 genes involved in immunity, cell differentiation, inflammation, insulin secretion, blood pressure regulation, and calcium homeostasis. Deficiency is a global pandemic — affecting 1 billion people.

rows: Rickets (children), osteomalacia (adults), muscle weakness, bone pain, fractures
Reduced immunity, fatigue, depression risk, muscle weakness, impaired calcium absorption
Suboptimal — bone density, immune function, mood may be affected
Target range for most clinical benefits. Cancer prevention, immune function, cardiovascular
Hypercalcaemia, hypercalciuria. Rare — requires very high supplementation (>40,000 IU/day chronic)
Under supervision — target 125–175 nmol/L (Coimbra protocol in MS, vitiligo)

pearls.label: 🟡 D3 + K2 + Mg — The Triad
pearls.text: Vitamin D3 should always be combined with: K2 (MK-7) 100–200mcg — directs calcium to bone (osteocalcin carboxylation) and arteries (matrix Gla protein activation), preventing vascular calcification from high-dose Vit D. Magnesium 300–400mg — required for Vit D activation (25-OH-D → 1,25-OH2-D requires Mg-dependent enzyme CYP27B1). Low magnesium blocks Vit D conversion — patients who take Vit D without Mg may not respond adequately. D3 without K2 at high doses can raise serum calcium and deposit it in soft tissues — always prescribe together.

cards.title: B Vitamins — Complete Clinical Guide

rows: Pyruvate dehydrogenase cofactor — glucose → energy. Nerve membrane maintenance.
Wet beriberi (cardiac, oedema). Dry beriberi (peripheral neuropathy). Wernicke's encephalopathy (alcoholics) — MEDICAL EMERGENCY: confusion + ophthalmoplegia + ataxia.

Wernicke's: IV thiamine 500mg TID × 3 days BEFORE glucose (giving glucose first precipitates Wernicke's in deficient patients). Maintenance: 50–100mg/day. High-risk: alcoholics, bariatric surgery, anorexia.
FAD/FMN cofactor — electron transport. Activates B6, folate, B12. Antioxidant (glutathione reductase).
Angular cheilitis, magenta tongue, photophobia, seborrhoea, normocytic anaemia. Worsens with oral contraceptives (common depletion).
Migraine prophylaxis ★★★ 400mg/day — mitochondrial energy enhancement. Maintenance: 10–50mg. Food: eggs, liver, dairy, almonds.
NAD+/NADP+ (central metabolism). Niacin: vasodilation, lipids. Niacinamide: DNA repair, longevity (NAD+ precursor), skin barrier.
Pellagra: 4 Ds — Dermatitis (sun-exposed), Diarrhoea, Dementia, Death. Common in: corn-dependent diets, alcoholism, Hartnup disease.

Niacin (flush): lipids — 1–3g/day (cardiovascular). Niacinamide (no-flush): acne 4–5% topical ★★★, anxiety 500–1000mg, NAD+ support (combined with NR). Tryptophan → niacin (60:1 ratio — 60mg tryptophan = 1mg niacin).
Coenzyme A synthesis — fatty acid metabolism, steroid hormone synthesis, acetylcholine. "Anti-stress vitamin" — adrenal support.
Burning feet syndrome. Deficiency rare — found in most foods. Seen in: severe malnutrition, alcoholism, some anti-HIV drugs.
HPA axis support: 500mg BD. Adrenal recovery protocol. Wound healing (topical dexpanthenol — Bepanthen). Acne: pantothenic acid 10g/day in some protocols (large dose).
Amino acid metabolism, neurotransmitter synthesis (serotonin, dopamine, GABA, histamine). PLP (active P5P form) essential for 100+ enzymes.
Depression, peripheral neuropathy (painful), glossitis, dermatitis, microcytic anaemia. Depleted by: OCP ★★★, isoniazid, penicillamine, theophylline.

OCP-related depression/PMS: P5P 25–50mg BD ★★★. Morning sickness in pregnancy: B6 25mg TID ★★★ (first-line WHO recommendation). Neurotransmitter support: P5P (active form — superior to pyridoxine). Toxicity: >200mg/day long-term → peripheral neuropathy (paradoxically). Use P5P not pyridoxine for chronic use.
Carboxylase cofactor — fatty acid synthesis, gluconeogenesis. Gene expression regulation. Keratin production.
Hair thinning (most common presenting complaint — though biotin deficiency is overdiagnosed). Brittle nails, seborrhoeic dermatitis, conjunctivitis.

Hair/nail: 2.5–5mg/day. Note: biotin supplementation falsely elevates thyroid tests and troponin on immunoassay — advise patients to stop biotin 48–72hrs before blood tests. Raw egg whites contain avidin which binds biotin (cooking destroys avidin).
1-carbon metabolism, DNA synthesis, methylation. Neural tube development. Homocysteine remethylation.
Megaloblastic anaemia. Neural tube defects (maternal deficiency → spina bifida). Elevated homocysteine. Depression.

Pregnancy prevention: 400–800mcg methylfolate (NOT folic acid in MTHFR — see below). Megaloblastic anaemia: 5mg/day AFTER confirming B12 adequate. MTHFR C677T/A1298C: use L-methylfolate (5-MTHF) not folic acid.
Myelin synthesis (methylcobalamin). DNA synthesis (adenosylcobalamin). Methylation cycle.

Megaloblastic anaemia, subacute combined degeneration of cord (posterior columns + lateral), peripheral neuropathy, depression, cognitive decline. Serum B12 lab range is too low (>150 pmol/L) — functional deficiency at 150–300 pmol/L.

Methylcobalamin preferred (over cyanocobalamin — active form, no cyanide metabolite). Oral: 1000mcg/day. IM hydroxycobalamin: monthly if severe deficiency or absorption failure (atrophic gastritis, gastrectomy, Crohn's, metformin use).

cards.title: Genomic Nutrition — MTHFR & Variants

alerts.label: Nutrigenomics — Personalised Nutrition Based on Genetics
alerts.text: Individual genetic variants (SNPs — single nucleotide polymorphisms) alter nutrient metabolism, requirements, and risk of deficiency-related disease. MTHFR is the most clinically actionable example — but other variants (COMT, APOE, VDR, BCMO1) are increasingly relevant in naturopathic practice.

rows: Methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase — converts folic acid to 5-MTHF (active methylfolate)
Homozygous (TT): enzyme activity ↓ 70%. Heterozygous (CT): ↓ 40%. High homocysteine if folate/B12/B6 insufficient.

Use L-methylfolate (5-MTHF) — NOT folic acid (cannot convert). B12 methylcobalamin. Riboflavin (B2 is MTHFR enzyme cofactor). Check homocysteine. Affects: cardiovascular risk, NTD risk, depression, recurrent miscarriage, treatment-resistant depression.
Less severe effect than C677T alone. Compound heterozygous (C677T + A1298C): significant impairment.
Same approach as C677T. Compound heterozygotes: more aggressive methylfolate protocol. May need B2 co-factor supplementation.
Catechol-O-methyltransferase — degrades catecholamines (dopamine, adrenaline, noradrenaline) and oestrogen metabolites

Val/Val (fast COMT): rapid catecholamine breakdown → less anxious, lower oestrogen metabolites. Met/Met (slow COMT): slow breakdown → higher dopamine (better working memory but higher anxiety), elevated 4-OH oestrogen (breast cancer risk).

Slow COMT + high oestrogen: DIM 200mg, calcium D-glucarate, Vit B2 (COMT requires SAMe and B2 as cofactors). High dopamine from slow COMT: may explain caffeine sensitivity, stress reactivity. Avoid: catechol-rich herbs (green tea, quercetin in excess) in slow COMT.
Apolipoprotein E — lipid transport, neuronal repair, amyloid clearance
APOE4: 1 copy = 3× Alzheimer's risk. 2 copies = 12× risk. Also: ↑ LDL, ↑ cardiovascular risk, ↑ omega-3 requirements (needs more DHA for neurological benefit)

APOE4 carriers: DHA 3g/day (brain protection — APOE4 impairs DHA transport to brain). Curcumin 2g/day (amyloid-clearing). Fasting (ketone bodies bypass impaired glucose metabolism). Avoid: excessive saturated fat (↑ LDL dramatically in APOE4). Ketogenic diet benefit controversial in APOE4.
Vit D receptor — determines responsiveness to Vit D
Multiple VDR variants (FokI, BsmI, TaqI, ApaI). Some variants: ↓ receptor affinity → higher Vit D doses needed for same response. VDR variants associated with: Hashimoto's, MS, T1DM, some cancers.
Patients with VDR variants may need 5000–10,000 IU Vit D to achieve adequate 25(OH)D levels. Test 25(OH)D and titrate by blood level — not by dose alone.

pearls.label: 🟡 MTHFR Testing — Practical Guidance
pearls.text: MTHFR testing: available through NivoFit, HealthifyMe, SRL Diagnostics, and direct-to-consumer services in India. Cost: ₹2000–5000. More meaningful than testing: serum homocysteine (functional marker of methylation pathway efficiency — target <9 µmol/L). Elevated homocysteine + unexplained cardiovascular risk + depression + recurrent miscarriage + treatment-resistant depression = MTHFR testing and methylation protocol beneficial. Patient communication: "Your body needs a pre-activated form of folate — it's like your body's GPS needing the right format of map." Avoid catastrophising — MTHFR is a SNP, not a disease. With appropriate supplementation, outcomes are excellent.
